optionA.text: Caruncle completely absent; neurosetae simple hooks

optionB.text: Caruncle present, usually well developed; neurosetae otherwise
Body elongated with parallel sides and usually abruptly tapering anteriorly and posteriorly
Some setigers (anterior or posterior) without branchiae

optionA.text: Caruncle with three parallel longitudinal ridges

optionB.text: Caruncle long and folded with indistinct lateral folds

optionA.text: Eyes absent, first pair of branchiae larger than the following ones

optionB.text: Eyes present, first branchiae not larger than the following ones

optionA.text: Caruncle with high central ridge and two wide flattened lateral folds

optionB.text: Lateral lobes of caruncle small and hidden under the central ridge, or absent

optionA.text: Caruncle wedge-shaped, without crest and folds
Caruncle high, loosely plaited and rugose

optionB.text: Caruncle low, narrowly plaited with a crenulated plate

optionA.text: Caruncle small and inconspicuous, stretching through maximally three segments

optionB.text: Caruncle large and conspicuous, stretching through at least three segments

optionA.text: Branchiae present on all segments from the second or third

optionB.text: Branchiae limited to the anterior part of the body

optionA.text: First segment with large, anteriorly directed hooks
Caruncle longer than wide with a large smooth, sinuous median ridge nearly covering the narrow lateral parts

optionB.text: Caruncle about as long as wide or wider, median ridge, if present, narrow

optionA.text: Caruncle without distinct median ridge, with a few deep transverse folds

optionB.text: Caruncle with a distinct, smooth narrow ridge
